title: SPLIT Manga versus Anime Character treatment Asuka etc
display_title: SPLIT Manga versus Anime Character treatment Asuka etc
wikitext:
[]: SPLIT: Manga versus Anime: Character treatment (Asuka, etc) Post by LordEvan88 » Wed Apr 13, 2011 5:13 pm Split of this thread . Enjoy! - SSD toe mash wrote: Sigh... This has been explained many times, but the ep 14 clip show is COMPLETELY justified and is an ordinary thing in anime, especially the kind that run 2 cours. Hell even the high paced TTGL had a breather clip episode half way through. It's so people that are new to the show can jump right in and figure out what's going on more or less so they won't lose potential viewers, and it's probably much much cheaper this way.

[]: Post by Bagheera » Wed Apr 13, 2011 7:15 pm LordEvan88 wrote: I get why its there and I accept it. I suppose I have grown to like the Manga more over time which is a lot more streamlined. The series is great but it is a sloppy creature at times and were it not that a great deal of the action arc was devoted to character development I could honestly do without it.

[]: Post by LordEvan88 » Wed Apr 13, 2011 7:40 pm Bagheera wrote: Wait, wait, wait...the manga is streamlined? Sadamoto's "I don't do hell train bits and I don't know what the fuck to do with Asuka" creation is streamlined ? Don't get me wrong, it has its moments, but it's even more of a mess than the TV show.

[]: Post by BiQ » Wed Apr 13, 2011 7:45 pm LordEvan88 wrote: The one thing I have consistently heard about all forms of Evangelion is that 'They don't know how to use Asuka right.' So I am just assuming that nobody has any idea what to do with Asuka and just not take it personally when people get agitated about it.

[]: Post by Bagheera » Wed Apr 13, 2011 7:53 pm BiQ wrote: Debates about romance-retconning of DC vs. lack of such of OA aside, I have yet to hear anyone say that (their preferred version of) the TV anime didn't get Asuka right. So, I'd say that depressed!Anno actually "Got it".

[]: Post by LordEvan88 » Wed Apr 13, 2011 8:00 pm Alright, I apologize for the broad and sweeping statement. But I feel that above all other characters people get highly agitated about Asuka's portrayal. And I feel it was just fine in the manga. Then again I feel she is an unpleasant and abrasive person either way. (Just to clarify this does not mean I do not like her, I do, quite a bit.) But people are so overly picky about her its difficult to put praise on anything without them bringing her up.

[]: Post by BiQ » Wed Apr 13, 2011 9:41 pm LordEvan88 wrote: But I feel that above all other characters people get highly agitated about Asuka's portrayal.

[]: Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Wed Apr 13, 2011 10:38 pm BiQ wrote: Debates about romance-retconning of DC vs. lack of such of OA aside, I have yet to hear anyone say that (their preferred version of) the TV anime didn't get Asuka right. So, I'd say that depressed!Anno actually "Got it".

[]: Post by Reichu » Thu Apr 14, 2011 1:09 pm FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: You know, I'd ask you to explain yourself, and I might want to read your thoughts on her anyway

[]: Post by Bagheera » Thu Apr 14, 2011 7:16 pm LordEvan88 wrote: Alright, I apologize for the broad and sweeping statement. But I feel that above all other characters people get highly agitated about Asuka's portrayal. And I feel it was just fine in the manga. Then again I feel she is an unpleasant and abrasive person either way. (Just to clarify this does not mean I do not like her, I do, quite a bit.) But people are so overly picky about her its difficult to put praise on anything without them bringing her up.

[]: Post by LordEvan88 » Thu Apr 14, 2011 9:22 pm Bagheera wrote: Asuka's breakdown in the show was brutal, but it was hers . What I mean by that is that no part of her storyline was used to prop up another character (well, the hospital scene in Air, but apart from that). She got screwed over -- hard -- but generally not in a way that advanced Shinji's plotline. Note what happens in the aftermath of Armisael's attack -- she's stripped of her pilot status and wanders around, ultimately attempting suicide before Section 2 finds her. What does that have to do with Shinji? NOTHING! Now look at what happens after the Arael attack in the manga: she's in the hospital, and Shinji gets to angst over her. Yay. And then, against the Eva series? She fights well and is ultimately crushed. Her story, not Shinji's. In the manga? Too soon to tell, but as far as we can tell he's gonna rescue her, or at least get involved. Lame. This holds for the manga in general. Yes, it smooths over some rough edges, and arguably tells a more organic story, but it does so at the expense of the laser focused character development seen in the show. Shinji, Asuka, and even Rei had well-defined arcs in the series that stood on their own. In the manga all are eroded considerably, and I haven't seem much in the way of payoff to make it worth the loss (and honestly I see that with Rei and Shinji, too, and even Kaworu. It's just most noticeable and easiest to describe in Asuka's case).

[]: Post by Bagheera » Thu Apr 14, 2011 9:59 pm LordEvan88 wrote: So basically your argument is that an action is justified when developing a single character you like but that very same action is 'Lame' when it is used to improve a separate character and overall improve the core arc of the story?

[]: Post by Kendrix » Fri Apr 15, 2011 7:53 am I think of Sadamoto's Manga as one of the WORST adaptations, if you ask me. It just lacks the soul of it all. There's no subtlety in anything, the characters are killing each other all over the place - The ones in NGE were disturbed, but still belieavable, It was possible for a normal human being to relate to them. The Manga is just overdramatic. I didn't think Asuka got it that bad, (Or well, maybe it's more apparent to her fans. It feels incredibly wrong for her to be artificial in any way, tough, her "old" backstory was way better - there's no impact to the stepchild complex if the guy isn't her actual father.), look at Ritsuko. I think she's creepy and insane in the original, but she was so because her creepyness remained hidden until the end - I don't think anyone would offer her Manga version a job. And let's not even talk about Kaworu. He was a completely different character, more like a crazy stalker. I get thsat Sadamoto was trying to make him more "inhuman" but he failed. We get to see too much from his perspective for that. The anime one was more of an overearthly, seemingly-omniscient creature, an intellectual young man who belonged to the realm of the abstract and had an uncanny abilities to read people like books. It's all full of stereotypic dialogue (think of the "made of straw" scene), they're killing all the mysteries with each dump of BG info, and they just can't do any subtleness - take the episode 20 equivalent as an example. Now, the actions of Manga!Shinji don't seem to fit together, and I just don't like how they made him more agressive. He just comes off as a whiny, always-complaining brat here. Also, Rei. She's missing something, like her, "strong" component or that sense of nonchalance for her situation she had at first. Just compare it to Rebuild - There, she gets defrosted, too, but shje doesn't just get "emotional", she displays a definite personality we already saw glimpses of in the anime, particularly in the dummy plug arc (15 to 19)

[]: Post by Shogo-Kun » Fri Apr 15, 2011 8:15 am I think that everyone is forgetting one crucial fact: IT'S NOT MEANT TO BE THE SAME STORY. If the manga followed the anime to a tee, it would feel hollow and lifeless. Why not just create an ani-manga if we're gonna go that route?

[]: Post by Eva Yojimbo » Fri Apr 15, 2011 8:16 am Reichu wrote: I'd probably have to give it some thought to explain properly... so you'll have to make do with this for now. I've classically disliked Rei, as well, though with the more subdued Rei-worship around here, it seems that those fires aren't fueled quite so much.

[]: Post by BornIn1142 » Fri Apr 15, 2011 8:30 am LordEvan88 wrote: Shinji's arc is definitely enhanced in the manga

[]: Post by Reichu » Fri Apr 15, 2011 10:23 am BornIn1142 wrote: Asuka fans have to put up with a lot.

[]: Post by BiQ » Fri Apr 15, 2011 10:55 am LordEvan88 wrote: the only thing lost seems to be a bit of Asuka. Quite a sin in your eyes I imagine, but a small price to pay for everything gained.

[]: Post by Bagheera » Fri Apr 15, 2011 11:08 am Kendrix wrote: I think of Sadamoto's Manga as one of the WORST adaptations, if you ask me. It just lacks the soul of it all.

[]: Post by InstrumentalityOne » Fri Apr 15, 2011 11:44 am The characters in the manga are probably still the same characters in the manga, it´s just that the manga is less ambiguous with things and more direct, I guess. Which kinda destroyed the whole "introverted/inner pains" thing.
